title: Chrysogonum repens
taxonomy: Chrysogonum repens is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a low-growing, spreading perennial native to the southeastern United States. The plant is commonly known as green and gold due to its bright green leaves and yellow flowers. Chrysogonum repens is often found in woodland edges, rocky outcroppings, and sandy areas. It is a member of the tribe Helenieae within the aster family.
uses: Chrysogonum repens, also known as green and gold or goldenstar, is a flowering plant native to the southeastern United States. Chrysogonum repens has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly by Native American communities, for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems. The root of Chrysogonum repens has been used in traditional medicine to treat fever and rheumatism. However, more research is needed to confirm the plant's medicinal properties and potential health benefits.

intro: Chrysogonum repens, also known as Green Dragon, is a perennial flowering plant native to North America, characterized by its small yellow flowers and creeping stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Chrysogonum repens include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Chrysogonum repens is typically prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is often used in herbal medicine to treat a range of conditions, including skin irritations, colds, and flu.
